channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: War Damage in Iran Exposes Dangerous Air Pollution and Health Risks
item.description: Recent U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran, alongside Iranian retaliatory missile attacks, have caused not only destruction but also severe air pollution. Fires from bombed oil depots and industrial s...

item.title: Government rolls out P5,000 cash aid for public transport drivers due to rising oil prices
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. spearheaded the distribution of P5,000 cash assistance to public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers who have been heavily impacted by recent oil price hikes. The f...

item.title: Supreme Court Agrees to Hear Arguments on Haitian and Syrian TPS Protections
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has announced it will hear oral arguments regarding the Trump administration’s attempt to terminate Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Haitians and Syrians. This move brings t...

item.title: Kenya Medical Association Raises Concerns Over Arrest of Nairobi Hospital Board Chair
item.description: The Kenya Medical Association (KMA) has strongly condemned the arrest of Dr. Obwaka, the Chair of the Nairobi Hospital Board. In a statement issued by its National Executive, KMA President Simon Kigun...

item.title: Ex-SAPS Task Force Member Charged in Murder of Madlanga Commission Witness
item.description: Matipandile Sotheni, 41, a former member of South Africa's police special task force, appeared in the Brakpan Magistrate's Court on 16 February 2026, facing multiple charges including murder, attempte...

item.title: SEC Proposes Allowing Public Companies to Report Earnings Twice a Year
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is exploring a proposal that would allow public companies to report their earnings twice a year instead of the traditional quarterly schedule. The dis...

item.title: Chennai man drives from Doha to Riyadh amid flight disruptions to return home
item.description: A Chennai resident, Varun Krishnan, faced a harrowing ordeal when his flight from Chennai to Barcelona got stranded in Doha due to escalating tensions in the Middle East involving Iran, the US, and Is...

item.title: Multiple Explosions Cause Fear in Maiduguri, Security Agencies Yet to Respond
item.description: On Monday evening, several explosions shook Maiduguri, Borno State, sending people waka for safety across different parts of the city. Sources inside the city tell say the blasts happen around 6:45 p....

item.title: Amnesty Calls for Full Investigation into Deadly Airstrike on Iranian School
item.description: Amnesty International has conducted a detailed investigation into a tragic airstrike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Iran, which resulted in approximately 168 fatalities, mostly children. Th...

item.title: Kenya and Russia Agree to Stop Recruiting Kenyans for Ukraine Conflict
item.description: Kenya don talk say e don reach agreement with Russia make Kenyans no longer fit enlist to fight for Russia for Ukraine. More than 1,000 Kenyans had been recruited, attracted by high salaries and bonus...

item.title: Western Allies Express Hesitation on NATO Involvement in Hormuz Strait
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump recently urged NATO to assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime route for global oil shipments, which has faced intermittent tensions. However, ...

item.title: KRA Penalties for Late or Non-Filing of Tax Returns in 2026
item.description: Every Kenyan with a KRA PIN is required to file tax returns annually. The Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) enforces this to ensure proper collection of taxes which fund government projects like roads, sc...

item.title: Kennedy Center to Close for Two-Year, $200 Million Renovation Project Approved by Board
item.description: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in Washington, D.C., is set to close for two years to undergo extensive renovations estimated to cost around $200 million. The board of the Kennedy C...

item.title: UK Government to Provide Targeted Aid to Households Facing Heating Oil Price Spike
item.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is set to introduce targeted support for households most affected by recent spikes in energy costs, particularly for families dependent on heating oil. The price of ...

item.title: How South Australia Regulates Truth in Political Advertising
item.description: South Australia enforces a unique law under Section 113 of the Electoral Act 1985 that makes it illegal for electoral advertisements to contain statements of fact that are materially inaccurate or mis...

item.title: Iranian Families Mourn Loved Ones Killed in US-Israeli Attacks in Tehran
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran, which began on February 28 with coordinated air strikes on Tehran and other cities, has so far claimed over 1,300 Iranian lives, according to officials. Families of the deceas...

item.title: Spotify Reports ₦60 Billion Earnings for Nigerian Artists in 2025 from 30.3 Billion Streams
item.description: For 2025, Nigerian artistes dey shine well-well as Spotify report show say dem don make over ₦60 billion from streaming alone. Over 30.3 billion streams and 1.6 billion listening hours dey confirm say...

item.title: Arusha man arrested for alleged sexual abuse of his two young daughters
item.description: Police in Arusha have arrested a 38-year-old motorcycle taxi rider, Yasini Ramadhan, for allegedly sexually abusing his two daughters, aged six years and seven months. The incident reportedly started ...

item.title: Sydney police provide statement after armed threat to woman in car park
item.description: Police in Sydney's western suburbs held a media briefing following an incident in which a woman was threatened by a man carrying a firearm in a local car park. Authorities confirmed that no shots were...

item.title: Rulani Mokwena receives suspended sentence and fine in Algeria for undeclared funds
item.description: South African football coach Rulani Mokwena was handed a two-month suspended prison sentence by Algerian authorities after being caught with undeclared foreign currency at Houari Boumediene Airport. T...
